sections.label: Section 959
sections.original: οὐ τῶν γάμων ἕκατι — μυρίαι κόραι
sections.translation: It is not to secure a bride that I have spoken thus — there are maids unnumbered

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: θηρῶσι λέκτρον τοὐμόν — εἴρηται τόδε ·
sections.translation: eager to have my love [*] — no ! but king Agamemnon has put an insult on me ; he should have asked my leave to use my name as a means to catch the child , for it was I [*] chiefly who induced Clytemnestra to betroth her daughter to me ;

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: ἔδωκά τἂν Ἕλλησιν , εἰ πρὸς Ἴλιον
sections.translation: I would had yielded this to Hellas , if that was where our going to Ilium broke down ; I would never have refused to further my fellow soldiers ’ common interest . But as it is , I am as nothing in the eyes of those chieftains , and little they care of treating me well or ill .

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: τάχʼ εἴσεται σίδηρος , ὃν πρὶν ἐς Φρύγας
sections.translation: My sword shall soon know if any one is to snatch your daughter from me , for then will I make it reek with the bloody stains of slaughter , before it reach Phrygia . [*] Calm yourself then ; as a god in his might I appeared to you , without being so , but such will I show myself for all that .

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: ἔλεξας , ὦ παῖ Πηλέως , σοῦ τʼ ἄξια
sections.translation: Son of Peleus , your words are alike worthy of you and that sea-born deity , the holy goddess .

sections.label: Section 977
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! would I could find words to utter your praise without excess , and yet not lose the graciousness of it by stinting it ; for when the good are praised , they have some sort of feeling

sections.label: Section 980
sections.original: μισοῦσι τοὺς αἰνοῦντας , ἢν αἰνῶσʼ ἄγαν .
sections.translation: of hatred for those who in their praise exceed the mean . But I am ashamed of intruding a tale of woe , since my affliction touches myself alone and you are not affected by troubles of mine ; but still it looks well for the man of worth to assist the unfortunate , even when he is not connected with then .

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: οἴκτιρε δʼ ἡμᾶς · οἰκτρὰ γὰρ πεπόνθαμεν ·
sections.translation: Therefore pity us , for our suffering cries for pity ; in the first place , I have harbored an idle hope , in thinking to have you marry my daughter ; and next , perhaps , the slaying of my child will be to you an evil omen in your wooing hereafter , against which you must guard yourself .

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὖ μὲν ἀρχὰς εἶπας , εὖ δὲ καὶ τέλη ·
sections.translation: Your words were good , both first and last ; for if you will it so , my daughter will be saved .

sections.label: Section 992
sections.original: βούλῃ νιν ἱκέτιν σὸν περιπτύξαι γόνυ ;
sections.translation: Will you have her clasp your knees as a suppliant ? it is no maid ’ s part ; yet if it seems good to you , why , come she shall with the modest look of free-born maid ;

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: εἰ δʼ οὐ παρούσης ταὐτὰ τεύξομαι σέθεν ,
sections.translation: but if I shall obtain the same end from you without her coming then let her abide within , for there is dignity in her reserve ; still reserve must only go as far as the case allows .

sections.label: Section 998
sections.original: σὺ μήτε σὴν παῖδʼ ἔξαγʼ ὄψιν εἰς ἐμήν ,
sections.translation: [*] Do not bring your daughter out for me to see , lady , nor let us incur the reproach of the ignorant ;

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: στρατὸς γὰρ ἀθρόος , ἀργὸς ὢν τῶν οἴκοθεν ,
sections.translation: for an army , when gathered together without domestic duties to employ it , loves the evil gossip of malicious tongues . After all , should you both supplicate me , you will attain a like result as if I had never been supplicated ; [*] for I am myself engaged in a mighty struggle to rid you of your troubles .

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: ὡς ἕν γʼ ἀκούσασʼ ἴσθι , μὴ ψευδῶς μʼ ἐρεῖν ·
sections.translation: One thing be sure you have heard ; I will not tell a lie ; if I do that or idly mock you , may I die , but live if I preserve the girl .

sections.label: Section 1008
sections.original: ὄναιο συνεχῶς δυστυχοῦντας ὠφελῶν .
sections.translation: Bless you for always helping the distressed !

sections.label: Section 1009
sections.original: ἄκουε δή νυν , ἵνα τὸ πρᾶγμʼ ἔχῃ καλῶς .
sections.translation: Hearken then to me , that the matter may succeed .

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: τί τοῦτʼ ἔλεξας ; ὡς ἀκουστέον γέ σου .
sections.translation: What is your proposal ? for hear you I must .

sections.label: Section 1011
sections.original: πειθώμεθʼ αὖτις πατέρα βέλτιον φρονεῖν .
sections.translation: Let us once more urge [*] her father to a better frame of mind .

sections.label: Section 1012
sections.original: κακός τίς ἐστι καὶ λίαν ταρβεῖ στρατόν .
sections.translation: He is something of a coward , and fears the army too much .

sections.label: Section 1013
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οἱ λόγοι γε καταπαλαίουσιν λόγους .
sections.translation: Still argument overthrows argument .

sections.label: Section 1014
sections.original: ψυχρὰ μὲν ἐλπίς · ὅ τι δὲ χρή με δρᾶν φράσον .
sections.translation: Cold hope indeed ; but tell me what I must do .

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: ἱκέτευʼ ἐκεῖνον πρῶτα μὴ κτείνειν τέκνα ·
sections.translation: Supplicate him first not to slay his children ; and if he is stubborn , come to me . For [*] if he consents to your request , my intervention need go no further , since this consent insures your safety . I too shall show myself in a better light to my friend ,

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: στρατός τʼ ἂν οὐ μέμψαιτό μʼ , εἰ τὰ πράγματα
sections.translation: and the army will not blame me , if I arrange the matter by reason rather than force ; while , should things turn out well , the result will prove satisfactory both to you and your friends , even without my interference .

sections.label: Section 1024
sections.original: ὡς σώφρονʼ εἶπας . δραστέον δʼ ἅ σοι δοκεῖ .
sections.translation: How sensibly you speak ! I must act as seems best to you ;

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: ἢν δʼ αὖ τι μὴ πράσσωμεν ὧν ἐγὼ θέλω ,
sections.translation: but should I fail of my object , where am I to see you again , where ? Must I turn my wretched steps and find you ready to champion my distress ?

sections.label: Section 1028
sections.original: ἡμεῖς σε φύλακες οὗ χρεὼν φυλάξομεν ,
sections.translation: I will keep watch to guard you , where occasion calls , that none may see you passing through the army of Danaids

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: Δαναῶν διʼ ὄχλου · μηδὲ πατρῷον δόμον
sections.translation: with that scared look . Do not shame your father ’ s house ; for Tyndareus does not deserve not to be ill spoken of , being a mighty man in Hellas . [*]

sections.label: Section 1033
sections.original: ἔστιν τάδʼ · ἄρχε · σοί με δουλεύειν χρεών .
sections.translation: It will be so . [*] Command me ; I must play the slave to you . If there are gods , you for your righteous dealing

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: ἐσθλῶν κυρήσεις · εἰ δὲ μή , τί δεῖ πονεῖν ;
sections.translation: will find them favorable ; if there are none , what need to toil ? Exeunt Achilles and Clytemnestra .

sections.label: Section 1.1036
sections.original: τίνʼ ἄρʼ Ὑμέναιος διὰ λωτοῦ Λίβυος
sections.translation: What wedding-hymn was that which raised its strains to the sound of Libyan flutes , to the music of the dancer ’ s lyre , and the note of the pipe of reeds ?

sections.label: Section 1.1040
sections.original: ὅτʼ ἀνὰ Πήλιον αἱ καλλιπλόκαμοι
sections.translation: It was on the day Pieria ’ s lovely-haired choir came over the slopes of Pelion to the wedding of Peleus , beating the ground with print of golden sandals at the banquet of the gods ,

sections.label: Section 1.1045
sections.original: μελῳδοῖς Θέτιν ἀχήμασι τόν τʼ Αἰακίδαν ,
sections.translation: and hymning in dulcet strains the praise of Thetis and the son of Aeacus , over the Centaurs ’ hill , down woods of Pelion .

sections.label: Section 1.1049
sections.original: ὁ δὲ Δαρδανίδας , Διὸς
sections.translation: There was the Dardanian boy ,

sections.label: Section 1.1050
sections.original: λέκτρων τρύφημα φίλον ,
sections.translation: dainty morsel of Zeus ’ bed , drawing off the wine he mixed in the depths of golden bowls , Ganymede the Phrygian ; while , along the gleaming sand ,

sections.label: Section 1.1055
sections.original: εἱλισσόμεναι κύκλια
sections.translation: the fifty daughters of Nereus graced the marriage with their dancing , circling in a mazy ring .

sections.label: Section 1.1058
sections.original: ἀνὰ δʼ ἐλάταισι στεφανώδει τε χλόᾳ
sections.translation: The revel-rout of Centaurs came too , mounted on horses , to the feast of the gods and the mixing-bowl of Bacchus ,

sections.label: Section 1.1060
sections.original: Κενταύρων ἐπὶ δαῖτα τὰν
sections.translation: leaning on fir-trees , with [*] wreaths of green foliage round their heads ; and Chiron cried loudly : Daughter of Nereus , you shall bear a son , a dazzling light to Thessaly ; and the prophet ,

sections.label: Section 1.1065
sections.original: εἰδὼς γεννάσειν
sections.translation: skilled in arts inspired by Phoebus , gave his name ; for he shall come with an army of Myrmidon spearmen to the famous land of Priam ,

sections.label: Section 1.1070
sections.original: γαῖαν ἐκπυρώσων ,
sections.translation: to set it in a blaze , his body cased in a suit of golden mail forged by Hephaestus , a gift from his goddess-mother , from Thetis who bore him .

sections.label: Section 1.1075
sections.original: Θέτιδος , ἅ νιν ἔτικτεν .
sections.translation: Then the gods shed a blessing on the marriage of the high-born bride , who was first of Nereus ’ daughters , and on the wedding of Peleus .

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: — σὲ δʼ ἐπὶ κάρα στέψουσι καλλικόμαν
sections.translation: But the Argives will crown you , [*] wreathing the lovely tresses of your hair , like a pure , dappled [*] heifer brought from some rocky cave , and staining with blood your human throat ;

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: οὐ σύριγγι τραφεῖσαν οὐδʼ
sections.translation: though you were never reared among the piping and whistling of herdsmen , but at your mother ’ s side , to be decked as the bride of a son of Inachus . [*] Where now does the face of modesty

sections.label: Section 1090
sections.original: ἢ τὸ τᾶς Ἀρετᾶς ἔχει
sections.translation: or virtue have any strength ? [*] seeing that godlessness holds sway , and virtue is neglected by men and thrust behind them ,

sections.label: Section 1095
sections.original: Ἀνομία δὲ νόμων κρατεῖ ,
sections.translation: lawlessness over law prevailing , and mortals no [*] longer making common cause to keep the jealousy of gods from reaching them .

sections.label: Section 1098
sections.original: ἐξῆλθον οἴκων προσκοπουμένη πόσιν ,
sections.translation: I have come from the tent to look out for my husband , who went away and left its shelter long ago ;

sections.label: Section 1100
sections.original: ἐν δακρύοισι δʼ ἡ τάλαινα παῖς ἐμή ,
sections.translation: while my poor child , hearing of the death her father designs for her , is in tears , uttering in many keys her piteous lamentation . Catching sight of Agamamnon . It seems I was speaking of one not far away ; for there is Agamemnon ,

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.original: ἀνόσια πράσσων αὐτίχʼ εὑρεθήσεται .
sections.translation: who will soon be detected in the commission of a crime against his own child .

sections.label: Section 1106
sections.original: Λήδας γένεθλον , ἐν καλῷ σʼ ἔξω δόμων
sections.translation: Daughter of Leda , it is lucky I have found you outside the tent , to discuss with you in our daughter ’ s absence subjects not suited for the ears of maidens on the eve of marriage .

sections.label: Section 1109
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν , οὗ σοι καιρὸς ἀντιλάζυται ;
sections.translation: What critical moment is it that you are seizing ?

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.original: ἔκπεμπε παῖδα δωμάτων πατρὸς μέτα ·
sections.translation: Send the maiden out to join her father , for the lustral water stands there ready , and barley-meal to scatter with the hand on the cleansing flame , and heifers to be slain before the marriage , in honor of the goddess Artemis , their black blood spouting from them .

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.original: τοῖς ὀνόμασιν μὲν εὖ λέγεις , τὰ δʼ ἔργα σου
sections.translation: Though the words you use are good , I do not know how I am to name your deeds in terms of praise .

sections.label: Section 1117
sections.original: χώρει δέ , θύγατερ , ἐκτός — οἶσθα γὰρ πατρὸς
sections.translation: Come [*] forth , my daughter ; well you know what is in your father ’ s mind ; take the child Orestes , your brother , and bring him with you in the folds of your robe .

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: ἰδοὺ πάρεστιν ἥδε πειθαρχοῦσά σοι .
sections.translation: Behold ! she comes , in obedience to your summons . I will speak the rest for her and for myself .

sections.label: Section 1122
sections.original: τέκνον , τί κλαίεις , οὐδʼ ἔθʼ ἡδέως ὁρᾷς ,
sections.translation: My child , why do you weep and no longer look cheerfully ? why are you fixing your eyes upon the ground and holding your robe before them ?

sections.label: Section 1124
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! with which of my woes shall I begin ?

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.original: ἅπασι γὰρ πρώτοισι χρήσασθαι πάρα
sections.translation: for I may treat them all as first , or put them last or midway , anywhere . [*]

sections.label: Section 1127
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν ; ὥς μοι πάντες εἰς ἓν ἥκετε ,
sections.translation: What is it ? I find you all alike , confusion and alarm in every eye .

sections.label: Section 1129
sections.original: εἴφʼ ἃν ἐρωτήσω σε γενναίως , πόσι .
sections.translation: My husband , answer frankly the questions I ask you .

sections.label: Section 1130
sections.original: οὐδὲν κελευσμοῦ δεῖ σʼ · ἐρωτᾶσθαι θέλω .
sections.translation: There is no necessity to order me ; I am willing to be questioned .

sections.label: Section 1131
sections.original: τὴν παῖδα τὴν σὴν τήν τʼ ἐμὴν μέλλεις κτενεῖν ;
sections.translation: Do you mean to slay your child and mine ?
